takes.event_basis: <cite index="21-7">NVIDIA fiscal 2027 Q1 earnings May 20</cite>. <cite index="25-9">Hyperscaler capex guidance of ~$725B annually</cite>; <cite index="21-1">CEO expects $1 trillion from Blackwell and Vera Rubin processors across 2026-2027</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="26-24">Stage appears set for another blowout quarter given heavy hyperscaler spending, strong AMD/Intel results, and Nvidia's comparatively poor YTD stock performance vs. peers</cite>. Valuation at 26.5x forward earnings offers asymmetric risk/reward if guidance reaffirms $1T demand thesis.
takes.risks: Beat already baked into consensus estimates (~79% revenue growth); miss or cautious commentary on 2027 capex sustainability could trigger sharp pullback.

takes.event_basis: Q1 FY27 Earnings on 2026-05-22 (after market close). CEO Huang's participation in US-China summit signals potential export pathway recovery; hyperscaler capex spending on track for $725B+ combined annually.
takes.thesis: <cite index="31-8">CEO Huang's participation in US delegation summit with China suggests potential pathway for advanced chip exports, removing regulatory headwinds on Chinese demand.</cite> Combined with sustained AI capex momentum and <cite index="6-9">hyperscaler combined 2026 spending projections tracking near $725 billion, above earlier expectations,</cite> NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and raised guidance.
takes.risks: Geopolitical detente could reverse if US-Iran tensions escalate; inflation surprise may prompt Warsh Fed to signal rate-hold policy extending into H2 2026.

takes.event_basis: Intel diversification deal validation; path to WWDC (June 4) with expected AI announcements. <cite index="47-4">Apple hit all-time intraday high of $290.33</cite>, now at $300.
takes.thesis: <cite index="32-5">Deal would gain steady stream of demand for Intel foundry, key milestone for contract manufacturing</cite>. WWDC catalyst sets up product strategy for 2026-27 iPhone/Mac cycle with manufacturing optionality. Supply chain diversification reduces TSMC concentration risk.
takes.risks: Stock at all-time highs with sentiment elevated; any product announcement shortfall or delayed Intel ramp could trigger profit-taking.

takes.event_basis: Apple foundry deal in preliminary stage; <cite index="37-3">analyst reports suggest Intel would focus on entry-level M7 and A21 chips, with production potentially starting late 2027</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="38-1">Deal suggests Intel 18A process has reached maturity to meet Apple standards, potentially opening door to additional high-profile foundry customers</cite>. <cite index="40-9">BofA estimates deal would add $10B annual sales by 2030</cite>. U.S. government backing and geopolitical momentum support execution.
takes.risks: <cite index="38-21">Deal remains preliminary with neither company providing official confirmation or timeline details</cite>; foundry segment remains loss-making and execution risk is high.
